meta.title: Toolbox Talk — Working at Height: Re-roofing Bay 3
meta.subtitle: Mixed crew · cherry-picker out of service · <5 min
meta.author: Site Supervisor
meta.deid_notice: Any referenced incident is de-identified — personnel are referred to by role label only; no names, no small (<5) injury cells, and no re-identification key appear in this talk.

sections.text: We are re-roofing Bay 3 this shift and the cherry-picker is out of service, so the crew is working off the leading edge by hand. A fall from this roof is a life-changing or fatal injury — this five-minute talk is about keeping everyone off that edge unprotected and home tonight.
The specific hazards on this task

items: Unprotected leading edge on the Bay 3 roof — fall from height (~6 m to the slab).
The cherry-picker (MEWP) is out of service, so the planned safe access is unavailable — work is being improvised at height.
Mixed crew — not everyone has worked this roof profile before; differing familiarity with the edge-protection layout.
Materials and tools at the edge — dropped-object risk to anyone below.

rows.control: Eliminate the leading-edge work — pre-assemble roof sections at ground level and lift them up as units where the task allows
Install a temporary edge-protection guardrail / scaffold edge run along the Bay 3 working edge before anyone goes near it
While the MEWP is down, restrict the work to inside the guardrail; anchored harness + running line for any task that must reach the edge
Permit-to-work + a briefed exclusion zone below; toe-boards / tethered tools for dropped-object control
Hard hats + harnesses worn and inspected — the LAST line, never the only line

sections.text: Learn from this: on a similar re-roofing job elsewhere, a worker stepped back toward an unguarded edge while carrying a sheet and was only stopped by a co-worker's shout. (Illustrative / typical example — not a specific local incident.) The lesson: edge protection goes up BEFORE the work starts, not after the first near-miss.

items: With the MEWP out of service, what is OUR safe way to reach the edge today — and what should we NOT attempt?
Where exactly is the edge protection going, and who checks it before we start?
If something feels wrong up there, what do you do — and who do you tell?
No one approaches the Bay 3 edge until the guardrail is up and checked.
MEWP is down — if a task needs the edge, it is harness + running line, or it waits.
Tools tethered, materials back from the edge, exclusion zone below kept clear.
See a hazard, stop the job and speak up — every one of us has stop-work authority.

sections.text: By signing, each crew member confirms they attended this briefing, understood the hazards and controls, and had the chance to raise concerns.

sections.caption: Toolbox-talk attendance / sign-off sheet — one row per attendee; the auditable record that the briefing happened.
